action_items.task: Fill out the last page of the LIHEAP application.
action_items.context: Necessary step to apply for utility assistance to prevent power shut-off.

action_items.task: Drop off the completed LIHEAP application after work.
action_items.context: Needed to secure funding for a $280 electric bill due next Thursday.

action_items.task: Look into emergency community funds if the client's LIHEAP application does not go through.
action_items.context: Backup plan to prevent the client's electric power from being shut off.

action_items.task: Complete a DOT physical at an urgent care clinic.
action_items.context: Mandatory requirement before the client can begin classes at the trucking academy.
Ongoing health requirement, specifically noted to ensure passing the blood pressure check for the DOT physical.

action_items.task: Abstain from all drugs and alcohol.
action_items.context: Standard ongoing condition restated by PO to protect the client's trucking academy opportunity.

critical_updates.details: Client continues to work at the Co-op, maintaining a steady 35 hours per week. He plans to keep his daytime shifts once his evening schooling begins.
Finances are tight, and the client received an electric shut-off notice for a $280 bill due next Thursday. He is currently applying for LIHEAP assistance to resolve the balance.
Client received an approval letter on Tuesday for full WIOA funding to attend the Tri-State Trucking Academy. He is enrolled in evening classes starting on the 28th.
Client reports his health is stable. He is consistently taking his daily prescribed Lisinopril to manage his blood pressure.
Client reports no recent police contact. He is spending his free time at home playing video games to avoid getting into trouble.
Client denies any drug or alcohol use. He expressed a strong commitment to maintaining sobriety to avoid jeopardizing his upcoming educational program.
Current meeting was held in the basement near holding cells due to lobby painting. The next scheduled meeting is set for the 12th at 9:00 AM.

next_episode_context: The client is working approximately 35 hours a week at the Co-op but faces short-term financial distress with a $280 electric shut-off notice. He is actively applying for LIHEAP to resolve it. In positive news, he secured full WIOA funding for the Tri-State Trucking Academy and begins evening classes on the 28th, pending a DOT physical next week. He remains compliant with no legal or substance issues and is managing his blood pressure daily with Lisinopril. Next meeting is scheduled for the 12th at 9:00 AM.
